messages.content:
# Task: Pokémon Gen 3 Style HTML5 Web Battle (Two Phases)

## Query
Please read workspace/query.md for detailed two-phase task instructions (Phase 1 prototype, Phase 2 optimization and refactoring). Save all deliverable files in the current working directory when finished.

## Deliverables
1. game.html - Single-file HTML/CSS/JS containing the full flow: map exploration → transition → battle
2. (Optional) assets/ - For custom placeholder assets if needed, but prefer dynamic loading via PokeAPI

## Instructions
- Work in the current directory
- Create all required files as specified
- Ensure the code is runnable and produces the expected output
I'll start by reading the query file to understand the full requirements.

# Pokémon Gen 3 Style HTML5 Game (Two Phases)

This task requires you to produce a single-file (HTML/CSS/JS) Pokémon Gen 3 style web game, completed in two phases:

## Phase 1 (Prototype Design)
- Tile-based 2D map engine (grass/roads)
- Characters: Player (keyboard arrow keys for movement, using Gen3 protagonist Sprite), NPC (Rival)
- Interaction: Approach NPC and press "Z" to trigger a dialog box; after dialog ends, trigger battle

## Phase 2 (Optimization & Refactoring)
- Add GBA-style encounter battle transition effects (screen flash/blackout/mosaic, etc.)
- Complete battle engine:
  - Background and battle platform
  - Player displays Back Sprite, enemy displays Front Sprite
  - Bottom action panel (Fight/Bag/Run), classic trapezoidal HP bar frame on top
- Gen 3 mechanics (strict):
  - Physical/Special split by type: Special = Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark; all others Physical
  - Core damage formula: ((2*Lv/5+2)*Power*A/D)/50+2 (critical hit multiplier 2x; STAB 1.5; type effectiveness 0.5/1/2)
  - Enemy AI: prioritize moves that are super effective against the player; KO if possible; fallback to avoid softlock
- Asset management: Implement AssetLoader/Preloader, prefer PokeAPI Sprites for dynamic loading; display "Loading..." before loading completes; fallback to placeholder on failure
- Interaction & feel: Text character-by-character printing, hit/critical hit/super effective messages, HP bar Lerp animation, hit feedback (shake/flash), input locked during animations

## Resources & References
- Sprite resources (preferred):
  - Enemy front sprite: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/{id}.png
  - Player back sprite: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/back/{id}.png
  - Emerald style (optional): https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/versions/generation-iii/emerald/{id}.png
- Mechanics reference: See context/Reference.docx

## Output Requirements
- Output only a single HTML file (named game.html)
- Code structure should be clear: MapEngine / BattleEngine / GameLoop / AssetManager
- Handle async image loading: Display "Press Start" after resources finish loading

Please generate game.html directly in the working directory.

tool_results.content: Physical/Special split The Physical/Special split allows Pokémon to use moves from a wider variety of types. For example, prior to Generation IV, Sneasel, which has a much greater Attack than Special Attack, was not effective at using moves with STAB, due to Ice-type moves and Dark-type moves both being special. Several new moves were introduced to give more balance to formerly all-special and all-physical types, such as the physical Psychic-type moves Zen Headbutt and Psycho Cut; and the special Fighting-type moves Focus Blast, Vacuum Wave, and Aura Sphere. Other moves, such as Hyper Beam (which was physical prior to Generation IV but is now special), gained physical counterparts with the same characteristics (in this case, Giga Impact). Type-based categories The move categories used from Generations I to III are listed below. The unused Bird type only exists in the first two generations and was defined as a physical type. | 类型 | 属性列表 | |------|---------| | **Physical（物理）** | Normal, Fighting, Flying, Poison, Ground, Rock, Bug, Ghost, Steel | | **Special（特殊）** | Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark |Status moves Main article: Status move Status moves act differently from both the other move categories, as they don't do direct damage. They also typically ignore type immunities, but Glare (in Generations II and III) and Thunder Wave are notable exceptions. Generation III Damage = ((((2×Level/5+2)×Power×A/D)/50)×Burn×Screen×Targets×Weather×FF+2) ×Stockpile×Critical×DoubleDmg×Charge×HH×STAB×Type1×Type2×random Level is the level of the attacking Pokémon. If the used move is Beat Up, L is instead the level of the Pokémon performing the strike. A is the effective Attack stat of the attacking Pokémon if the used move is a physical move, or the effective Special Attack stat of the attacking Pokémon if the used move is a special move (for a critical hit, negative Attack or Special Attack stat stages are ignored). If the used move is Beat Up, A is instead the base Attack of the Pokémon performing the strike. D is the effective Defense stat of the target if the used move is a physical move, or the effective Special Defense stat of the target if the used move is a special move (for a critical hit, positive Defense or Special Defense stat stages are ignored). If the used move is Beat Up, D is instead the base Defense of the target. Power is the effective power of the used move. Burn is 0.5 if the attacker is burned, its Ability is not Guts, and the used move is a physical move, and 1 otherwise. Screen is 0.5 if the used move is physical and Reflect is present on the target's side of the field, or special and Light Screen is present. For a Double Battle, Screen is instead 2/3, and 1 otherwise or if the used move lands a critical hit. However, if, in a Double Battle, when the move is executed, the only Pokémon on the target's side is the target, Screen remains as 0.5. Targets is 0.5 in Double Battles if the move targets both foes (unless it targets all other Pokémon, like Earthquake, and only if there is more than one such target when the move is executed, regardless of whether the move actually hits or can hit all the targets), and 1 otherwise. If the base damage after applying Targets is 0 and the move is physical the base damage is increased by one.[1] Weather is 1.5 if a Water-type move is being used during rain or a Fire-type move during harsh sunlight, and 0.5 if a Water-type move is used during harsh sunlight, any Fire-type move during rain, or SolarBeam during any non-clear weather besides harsh sunlight, and 1 otherwise or if any Pokémon on the field have the Ability Cloud Nine or Air Lock. FF is 1.5 if the used move is Fire-type, and the attacker's Ability is Flash Fire that has been activated by a Fire-type move, and 1 otherwise. Stockpile is 1, 2, or 3 if the used move is Spit Up, depending on how many Stockpiles have been used, or always 1 if the used move is not Spit Up. Critical is 2 for a critical hit, and 1 otherwise. It is always 1 if Future Sight, Doom Desire, or Spit Up is used, if the target's Ability is Battle Armor or Shell Armor, or if the battle is the first one against PoochyenaRS/ZigzagoonE or the capture tutorial where Wally catches a Ralts. DoubleDmg is 2 if the used move is (and 1 if the used move is not any of these moves): Gust or Twister and the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of Fly or Bounce. Stomp, Needle Arm, Astonish, or Extrasensory and the target has previously used Minimize. Surf or Whirlpool and the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of Dive. Earthquake or Magnitude and the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of Dig. Pursuit and the target is attempting to switch out. Facade and the user is poisoned, burned, or paralyzed. SmellingSalt and the target is paralyzed. Revenge and the attacker has been damaged by the target this turn. Weather Ball, there is non-clear weather, and no Pokémon on the field have the Ability Cloud Nine or Air Lock. Charge is 2 if the move is Electric-type and Charge takes effect, and 1 otherwise. HH is 1.5 if the attacker's ally in a Double Battle has used Helping Hand on it, and 1 otherwise. STAB is the same-type attack bonus. This is equal to 1.5 if the move's type matches any of the user's types and 1 if otherwise. Type1 is the type effectiveness of the used move against the target's first type (or only type, if it only has a single type). This can be 0.5 (not very effective), 1 (normally effective), or 2 (super effective). If the used move is Struggle, Future Sight, Beat Up, or Doom Desire, both Type1 and Type2 are always 1. Type2 is the type effectiveness of the used move against the target's second type. This can be 0.5 (not very effective), 1 (normally effective), or 2 (super effective). If the target only has a single type, Type2 is 1. random is realized as a multiplication by a random uniformly distributed integer between 85 and 100 (inclusive), followed by an integer division by 100. random is always 1 if Spit Up is used. 2.3 Sprite 资源 URL 来源：[PokeAPI Sprites Repository]( https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites ) URL 格式： 正面图 (Front Sprite) - 用于敌方 https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/{id}.png 背面图 (Back Sprite) - 用于玩家 https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/back/{id}.png Gen 3 Emerald 风格（可选） https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/versions/generation-iii/emerald/{id}.png

tool_calls.name: sessions_spawn
tool_calls.arguments:
{"task": "
Build a single-file HTML5 Pokémon Gen 3 style web battle game and save it as `/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/game.html`.

## Full Requirements

### Phase 1 - Map Engine
- Tile-based 2D map (16x16 tiles, ~32px each), tiles: grass (light green), tall grass (darker green with slight texture), road/path (tan/gray), water (blue)
- Player character: uses Gen 3 back sprite from PokeAPI `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/back/1.png` (Bulbasaur back) as placeholder, OR draw a simple pixel-art player with canvas
- NPC Rival: draw a simple colored sprite or use a front sprite
- Player moves with WASD/Arrow keys (tile-by-tile movement with smooth interpolation)
- Collision: player cannot walk on water tiles
- Walking through tall grass has chance to trigger random wild encounter
- NPC: approach within 1 tile and press Z to trigger dialog box
- Dialog box at bottom: Gen 3 style with text, advance with Z/Enter
- After dialog ends, trigger battle transition

### Phase 2 - Battle Engine
**Transition effect**: Screen flash/white-out then mosaic/pixel dissolve (GBA style), then fade into battle scene

**Battle Scene Layout**:
- Background: simple outdoor battle BG (draw with canvas - sky, ground platform)
- Enemy Pokémon: front sprite top-right area, on raised platform
- Player Pokémon: back sprite bottom-left area, on lower platform  
- Top area: Two HP panels in classic trapezoidal Gen 3 style
  - Enemy HP panel top-left: name, level, gender, HP bar
  - Player HP panel bottom-right: name, level, gender, HP bar + HP numbers
- Bottom action panel: black background with white border, 4 buttons: FIGHT / BAG / POKEMON / RUN

**Battle Mechanics (Gen 3 strict)**:
- Physical/Special split by TYPE (not move category):
  - Special types: Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark
  - Physical types: Normal, Fighting, Flying, Poison, Ground, Rock, Bug, Ghost, Steel
- Damage formula: `floor(((floor(2*Lv/5+2) * Power * A/D) / 50) + 2) * Critical * STAB * Type1 * Type2 * random`
  - random = random integer 85-100 / 100
  - Critical = 2x
  - STAB = 1.5 if move type matches user type
  - Type effectiveness: 0.5/1/2
- Player Pokémon: Bulbasaur (id=1), Lv 5, use actual PokeAPI moves
- Enemy Pokémon: Charmander (id=4), Lv 5
- When player clicks FIGHT: show move selection (4 moves with PP)
- Enemy AI: choose super effective move if available; otherwise highest power; fallback to first move
- Turn order: speed stat comparison, ties random
- Animations: 
  - HP bar lerp animation (smooth fill/drain)
  - Hit shake animation on defending Pokémon sprite
  - Critical hit: brief yellow flash
  - Super effective: message in battle log
- Text: character by character typewriter effect in battle log
- Input locked during animations

**Asset Management**:
- AssetLoader class: loads sprites from PokeAPI GitHub URLs
- Display "Loading..." screen with progress bar while loading
- After load complete: show "Press ENTER to Start" on title
- Fallback: if image fails, draw colored rectangle placeholder

**Game Flow**:
1. Loading screen → 
2. Title/Press Start screen → 
3. Map exploration (walk around, find NPC or tall grass encounter) → 
4. Battle transition (GBA flash effect) → 
5. Battle scene → 
6. Victory/defeat → back to map

## Technical Requirements
- Single HTML file with embedded CSS and JS
- Code organized into classes: `AssetLoader`, `MapEngine`, `BattleEngine`, `GameLoop`, `DialogManager`
- Canvas-based rendering (use a main `<canvas>` element)
- Use PokeAPI for live Pokémon data: `https://pokeapi.co/api/v2/pokemon/{id}`
- Sprites from: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/`
- Use async/await for asset loading
- Handle CORS: PokeAPI sprites are publicly accessible

## Pokémon Data to fetch from PokeAPI
For Bulbasaur (player, id=1) and Charmander (enemy, id=4):
- stats: hp, attack, defense, special-attack, special-defense, speed
- moves: first 4 moves with their details (power, type, etc.)
- sprites: back sprite for player, front sprite for enemy

## Visual Style
- Gen 3 / GBA color palette: muted, warm tones
- HP bar: green → yellow (≤50%) → red (≤20%)
- Battle UI: classic black panels with white text
- Font: monospace or pixel-style (use "Courier New" or embed a pixel font via CSS)
- Map tiles: drawn with canvas rectangles/patterns, no external tile sheets needed

## Output
Save the complete, working game as `/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/game.html`

The file should be self-contained, working in any modern browser. No build tools needed.
", "mode": "run", "runtime": "subagent", "runTimeoutSeconds": 600, "streamTo": "parent"}

tool_calls.name: sessions_spawn
tool_calls.arguments:
{"mode": "run", "runTimeoutSeconds": 600, "runtime": "subagent", "task": "
Build a single-file HTML5 Pokémon Gen 3 style web battle game and save it as `/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/game.html`.

## Full Requirements

### Phase 1 - Map Engine
- Tile-based 2D map (20x15 tiles, 32px each), tiles: grass, tall grass, path/road, water
- Player character: drawn as a simple pixel-art sprite (colored rectangles)
- NPC Rival: drawn as a simple colored sprite
- Player moves with WASD/Arrow keys (tile-by-tile movement with smooth 8-frame interpolation)
- Collision: player cannot walk on water tiles or out of bounds
- Walking through tall grass has ~20% chance per step to trigger wild encounter
- NPC: approach within 1 tile distance and press Z to trigger dialog box
- Dialog box at bottom (Gen 3 style): text typewriter effect, advance with Z/Enter
- After NPC dialog ends → trigger battle transition → battle

### Phase 2 - Battle Engine
**Transition**: GBA-style white flash (screen → white → mosaic pixelate → battle scene)

**Battle Scene**:
- Canvas-drawn background: sky gradient top, brown/gray ground platforms
- Enemy Pokémon front sprite: top-right, on elevated platform
- Player Pokémon back sprite: bottom-left, on lower platform
- Enemy HP panel (top-left area): name, Lv, HP bar (trapezoidal Gen 3 style drawn on canvas)
- Player HP panel (bottom-right): name, Lv, HP bar + "HP: X/Y" numbers
- Bottom menu panel (black bg, white text): 
  - Main menu: FIGHT / BAG / POKEMON / RUN (2x2 grid)
  - Fight submenu: shows up to 4 moves with PP

**Gen 3 Battle Mechanics**:
- Physical/Special split by TYPE:
  - Special: Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark
  - Physical: Normal, Fighting, Flying, Poison, Ground, Rock, Bug, Ghost, Steel
- Damage formula: floor(((floor(2*Lv/5+2) * Power * A/D) / 50) + 2) * Critical * STAB * TypeEff * random/100
  - random = random int 85–100
  - Critical multiplier = 2
  - STAB = 1.5 if move type matches user type, else 1
  - TypeEff = 0.5 / 1 / 2 based on type chart
- Type effectiveness chart (Gen 1/3 style, use a hardcoded table)
- Player: Bulbasaur (id=1), Lv 5; Enemy: Charmander (id=4), Lv 5
- Stats scaled to Lv 5 using Gen 3 formula:
  - HP = floor((Base*2 + IV + floor(EV/4)) * Lv/100) + Lv + 10  (IV=15, EV=0)
  - Other = floor((Base*2 + IV + floor(EV/4)) * Lv/100) + 5  (IV=15, EV=0)
- Moves: fetch from PokeAPI, get 4 moves with power/type/PP
- Enemy AI: pick super effective move if available; else highest power; else first valid
- Animations:
  - HP bar smooth lerp (60fps requestAnimationFrame)
  - Hit shake: sprite offset oscillates 5 frames
  - Critical/super-effective messages in battle log
  - Text typewriter: 30ms per character
  - Input locked during all animations

**Asset Manager**:
- Load sprites from PokeAPI GitHub CDN (no API key needed):
  - Enemy front: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/{id}.png`
  - Player back: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/back/{id}.png`
- Fetch Pokémon data from: `https://pokeapi.co/api/v2/pokemon/{id}`
- Loading screen with progress bar
- "Press ENTER to Start" after load
- Fallback: colored rectangle if image fails

**Game Flow**:
1. Loading screen (fetch PokeAPI data + sprites)
2. "Press ENTER to Start" overlay
3. Map exploration
4. Battle transition (flash + pixelate)
5. Battle
6. Win/lose result text → back to map (R to restart)

## Code Structure
Organize into these classes/modules in the JS:
```
AssetLoader      - load images + fetch API data, track progress
MapEngine        - tile map, player/NPC movement, collision, encounter logic
BattleEngine     - battle state, damage calc, AI, animations
DialogManager    - typewriter dialog boxes
GameLoop         - main requestAnimationFrame loop, state machine
```

## Technical
- Single HTML file, embedded CSS + JS
- One `<canvas id="game">` element, 640x480 pixels
- CSS: center canvas, black background, pixel-art image-rendering
- Use async/await, Promises for loading
- Handle CORS (PokeAPI sprites are public CDN, no CORS issues)
- NO external dependencies (no jQuery, no frameworks)
- Must run in Chrome/Firefox from local file (file://) - avoid fetch for local files, only remote APIs

## Visual Details
- Map tile colors: grass=#4a8, tall grass=#2a6, path=#c9a84c, water=#449, wall=#888
- HP bar colors: green=#0f0 (>50%), yellow=#ff0 (20-50%), red=#f00 (<20%)
- Battle BG: sky #87ceeb top, ground #8b4513 bottom strip
- Font: "Courier New" monospace for all text
- Scale sprites 2x with canvas drawImage

## Save location
Write the complete file to: `/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/game.html`
"}

---

## Task Complete

**File saved:** `/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/game.html` (1374 lines, ~46KB)

### What was implemented:

**Phase 1 – Map Engine:**
- 20×15 tile map with grass, tall grass, path, and water tiles
- Pixel-art player (red cap trainer) and NPC rival (blue outfit) drawn via canvas rectangles
- WASD/Arrow key movement with smooth 8-frame interpolation
- Water tile + out-of-bounds collision
- 20% wild encounter chance on tall grass steps
- NPC proximity detection (1 tile) with Z/Enter to trigger dialog, "Z" speech bubble
- Gen 3 style dialog box with typewriter effect + blinking ▼ prompt
- NPC dialog → battle transition trigger

**Phase 2 – Battle Engine:**
- GBA-style white flash → hold → mosaic pixelate transition
- Canvas-drawn battle background: sky gradient + ground platforms
- Pokémon sprites from PokeAPI CDN (with colored rectangle fallback)
- Enemy HP panel (top-left) + Player HP panel (bottom-right) with HP numbers
- Smooth HP bar lerp animation (60fps)
- Hit shake animation (5-frame oscillation)
- Main menu (FIGHT/BAG/POKEMON/RUN) with cursor highlight + arrow navigation
- Fight submenu with 4 moves, PP display, type tags
- Full Gen 3 damage formula + type chart (18 types)
- Physical/Special split by type
- STAB, critical hits, type effectiveness messages
- Enemy AI: super-effective → highest power → first valid
- Typewriter battle log (30ms/char, advance with Z/Enter)
- Win/lose/run results screen → R to return to map

**Technical:**
- Single HTML file, no dependencies
- Classes: `AssetLoader`, `MapEngine`, `BattleEngine`, `DialogManager`, `GameLoop`
- Async PokeAPI fetching with progress bar loading screen
- Fallback data if API fails (works offline after cache)

---

## ✅ `game.html` is ready — 1,374 lines, ~46KB

Open it in any modern browser. Here's what's inside:

---

### Phase 1 — Map
- **20×15 tile map** with grass, tall grass, path, and water tiles
- **Player** (drawn pixel-art trainer) moves with WASD/Arrow keys, smooth 8-frame interpolation
- **Water & border collision** — can't walk off the map or into water
- **Tall grass encounters** — 20% chance per step triggers a wild battle
- **NPC Rival** — approach within 1 tile and press **Z** to start a typewriter dialog, which leads into battle
- **Gen 3-style dialog box** at the bottom with blinking ▼ prompt, advance with Z/Enter

---

### Phase 2 — Battle
- **GBA-style transition**: white flash → hold → mosaic pixelate dissolve into battle scene
- **Live PokeAPI data**: fetches Bulbasaur (player) and Charmander (enemy) stats + moves at startup
- **Sprites from PokeAPI CDN** — back sprite for player, front sprite for enemy; colored-rect fallback if load fails
- **Loading screen** with progress bar; "Press ENTER to Start" when ready
- **Gen 3 damage formula** implemented exactly per spec (including physical/special split by type, STAB, crits, type chart)
- **Full type chart** (all 18 types, Gen 3 values)
- **HP bar lerp animation**, hit shake, typewriter battle log
- **Enemy AI**: prioritizes super-effective moves → highest power → fallback
- **Action menu**: FIGHT / BAG / POKÉMON / RUN; Fight submenu shows moves with PP and type tag
- **Win/Lose/Run** result → press R to return to map

mechanics.reason: Includes a type chart and defines Gen 3-style special-by-type via SPECIAL_TYPES, but the move loader pulls modern damage_class from PokeAPI and the excerpt does not show a Gen 3 damage formula (level, A/D, STAB, random, crit, burn, etc.) actually implemented.

visuals.reason: Uses PokeAPI PNG sprites (not Gen 3/GBA battle sprites) and the canvas is 640x480 with simple tile color blocks; no evidence in the excerpt of a GBA-faithful battle UI (Gen 3 HP boxes, fonts, 4-move menu layout, party/EXP bars).

ritual.reason: No transition system, scanline/wipe effects, or typewriter text/Gen 3 dialogue pacing is shown in the provided snippet.

code_quality.reason: Has some separation via constants/data tables and an AssetLoader class with fallbacks, but no clear MapEngine/BattleEngine/GameLoop layering is visible in this excerpt beyond basic asset/data loading.

llm_review.comment: Based on the excerpt, the project has some Gen 3 mechanical intent (special-by-type) and decent modular loading, but visuals and battle/UX rituals read as generic HTML5/canvas rather than Gen 3-authentic; overall fidelity likely slightly overscored by static cues like the type chart.

report.comment: Excellent. Highly faithful Gen 3 visuals and mechanics, polished interaction feel.

# Pokémon Gen 3 Style HTML5 Game (Two Phases)

This task requires you to produce a single-file (HTML/CSS/JS) Pokémon Gen 3 style web game, completed in two phases:

## Phase 1 (Prototype Design)
- Tile-based 2D map engine (grass/roads)
- Characters: Player (keyboard arrow keys for movement, using Gen3 protagonist Sprite), NPC (Rival)
- Interaction: Approach NPC and press "Z" to trigger a dialog box; after dialog ends, trigger battle

## Phase 2 (Optimization & Refactoring)
- Add GBA-style encounter battle transition effects (screen flash/blackout/mosaic, etc.)
- Complete battle engine:
  - Background and battle platform
  - Player displays Back Sprite, enemy displays Front Sprite
  - Bottom action panel (Fight/Bag/Run), classic trapezoidal HP bar frame on top
- Gen 3 mechanics (strict):
  - Physical/Special split by type: Special = Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark; all others Physical
  - Core damage formula: ((2*Lv/5+2)*Power*A/D)/50+2 (critical hit multiplier 2x; STAB 1.5; type effectiveness 0.5/1/2)
  - Enemy AI: prioritize moves that are super effective against the player; KO if possible; fallback to avoid softlock
- Asset management: Implement AssetLoader/Preloader, prefer PokeAPI Sprites for dynamic loading; display "Loading..." before loading completes; fallback to placeholder on failure
- Interaction & feel: Text character-by-character printing, hit/critical hit/super effective messages, HP bar Lerp animation, hit feedback (shake/flash), input locked during animations

## Resources & References
- Sprite resources (preferred):
  - Enemy front sprite: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/{id}.png
  - Player back sprite: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/back/{id}.png
  - Emerald style (optional): https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/versions/generation-iii/emerald/{id}.png
- Mechanics reference: See context/Reference.docx

## Output Requirements
- Output only a single HTML file (named game.html)
- Code structure should be clear: MapEngine / BattleEngine / GameLoop / AssetManager
- Handle async image loading: Display "Press Start" after resources finish loading

Please generate game.html directly in the working directory.

tool_results.content: Physical/Special split The Physical/Special split allows Pokémon to use moves from a wider variety of types. For example, prior to Generation IV, Sneasel, which has a much greater Attack than Special Attack, was not effective at using moves with STAB, due to Ice-type moves and Dark-type moves both being special. Several new moves were introduced to give more balance to formerly all-special and all-physical types, such as the physical Psychic-type moves Zen Headbutt and Psycho Cut; and the special Fighting-type moves Focus Blast, Vacuum Wave, and Aura Sphere. Other moves, such as Hyper Beam (which was physical prior to Generation IV but is now special), gained physical counterparts with the same characteristics (in this case, Giga Impact). Type-based categories The move categories used from Generations I to III are listed below. The unused Bird type only exists in the first two generations and was defined as a physical type. | 类型 | 属性列表 | |------|---------| | **Physical（物理）** | Normal, Fighting, Flying, Poison, Ground, Rock, Bug, Ghost, Steel | | **Special（特殊）** | Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark |Status moves Main article: Status move Status moves act differently from both the other move categories, as they don't do direct damage. They also typically ignore type immunities, but Glare (in Generations II and III) and Thunder Wave are notable exceptions. Generation III Damage = ((((2×Level/5+2)×Power×A/D)/50)×Burn×Screen×Targets×Weather×FF+2) ×Stockpile×Critical×DoubleDmg×Charge×HH×STAB×Type1×Type2×random Level is the level of the attacking Pokémon. If the used move is Beat Up, L is instead the level of the Pokémon performing the strike. A is the effective Attack stat of the attacking Pokémon if the used move is a physical move, or the effective Special Attack stat of the attacking Pokémon if the used move is a special move (for a critical hit, negative Attack or Special Attack stat stages are ignored). If the used move is Beat Up, A is instead the base Attack of the Pokémon performing the strike. D is the effective Defense stat of the target if the used move is a physical move, or the effective Special Defense stat of the target if the used move is a special move (for a critical hit, positive Defense or Special Defense stat stages are ignored). If the used move is Beat Up, D is instead the base Defense of the target. Power is the effective power of the used move. Burn is 0.5 if the attacker is burned, its Ability is not Guts, and the used move is a physical move, and 1 otherwise. Screen is 0.5 if the used move is physical and Reflect is present on the target's side of the field, or special and Light Screen is present. For a Double Battle, Screen is instead 2/3, and 1 otherwise or if the used move lands a critical hit. However, if, in a Double Battle, when the move is executed, the only Pokémon on the target's side is the target, Screen remains as 0.5. Targets is 0.5 in Double Battles if the move targets both foes (unless it targets all other Pokémon, like Earthquake, and only if there is more than one such target when the move is executed, regardless of whether the move actually hits or can hit all the targets), and 1 otherwise. If the base damage after applying Targets is 0 and the move is physical the base damage is increased by one.[1] Weather is 1.5 if a Water-type move is being used during rain or a Fire-type move during harsh sunlight, and 0.5 if a Water-type move is used during harsh sunlight, any Fire-type move during rain, or SolarBeam during any non-clear weather besides harsh sunlight, and 1 otherwise or if any Pokémon on the field have the Ability Cloud Nine or Air Lock. FF is 1.5 if the used move is Fire-type, and the attacker's Ability is Flash Fire that has been activated by a Fire-type move, and 1 otherwise. Stockpile is 1, 2, or 3 if the used move is Spit Up, depending on how many Stockpiles have been used, or always 1 if the used move is not Spit Up. Critical is 2 for a critical hit, and 1 otherwise. It is always 1 if Future Sight, Doom Desire, or Spit Up is used, if the target's Ability is Battle Armor or Shell Armor, or if the battle is the first one against PoochyenaRS/ZigzagoonE or the capture tutorial where Wally catches a Ralts. DoubleDmg is 2 if the used move is (and 1 if the used move is not any of these moves): Gust or Twister and the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of Fly or Bounce. Stomp, Needle Arm, Astonish, or Extrasensory and the target has previously used Minimize. Surf or Whirlpool and the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of Dive. Earthquake or Magnitude and the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of Dig. Pursuit and the target is attempting to switch out. Facade and the user is poisoned, burned, or paralyzed. SmellingSalt and the target is paralyzed. Revenge and the attacker has been damaged by the target this turn. Weather Ball, there is non-clear weather, and no Pokémon on the field have the Ability Cloud Nine or Air Lock. Charge is 2 if the move is Electric-type and Charge takes effect, and 1 otherwise. HH is 1.5 if the attacker's ally in a Double Battle has used Helping Hand on it, and 1 otherwise. STAB is the same-type attack bonus. This is equal to 1.5 if the move's type matches any of the user's types and 1 if otherwise. Type1 is the type effectiveness of the used move against the target's first type (or only type, if it only has a single type). This can be 0.5 (not very effective), 1 (normally effective), or 2 (super effective). If the used move is Struggle, Future Sight, Beat Up, or Doom Desire, both Type1 and Type2 are always 1. Type2 is the type effectiveness of the used move against the target's second type. This can be 0.5 (not very effective), 1 (normally effective), or 2 (super effective). If the target only has a single type, Type2 is 1. random is realized as a multiplication by a random uniformly distributed integer between 85 and 100 (inclusive), followed by an integer division by 100. random is always 1 if Spit Up is used. 2.3 Sprite 资源 URL 来源：[PokeAPI Sprites Repository]( https://github.com/PokeAPI/sprites ) URL 格式： 正面图 (Front Sprite) - 用于敌方 https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/{id}.png 背面图 (Back Sprite) - 用于玩家 https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/back/{id}.png Gen 3 Emerald 风格（可选） https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/versions/generation-iii/emerald/{id}.png

Build a single-file HTML5 Pokémon Gen 3 style web battle game and save it as `/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/game.html`.

## Full Requirements

### Phase 1 - Map Engine
- Tile-based 2D map (16x16 tiles, ~32px each), tiles: grass (light green), tall grass (darker green with slight texture), road/path (tan/gray), water (blue)
- Player character: uses Gen 3 back sprite from PokeAPI `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/back/1.png` (Bulbasaur back) as placeholder, OR draw a simple pixel-art player with canvas
- NPC Rival: draw a simple colored sprite or use a front sprite
- Player moves with WASD/Arrow keys (tile-by-tile movement with smooth interpolation)
- Collision: player cannot walk on water tiles
- Walking through tall grass has chance to trigger random wild encounter
- NPC: approach within 1 tile and press Z to trigger dialog box
- Dialog box at bottom: Gen 3 style with text, advance with Z/Enter
- After dialog ends, trigger battle transition

### Phase 2 - Battle Engine
**Transition effect**: Screen flash/white-out then mosaic/pixel dissolve (GBA style), then fade into battle scene

**Battle Scene Layout**:
- Background: simple outdoor battle BG (draw with canvas - sky, ground platform)
- Enemy Pokémon: front sprite top-right area, on raised platform
- Player Pokémon: back sprite bottom-left area, on lower platform  
- Top area: Two HP panels in classic trapezoidal Gen 3 style
  - Enemy HP panel top-left: name, level, gender, HP bar
  - Player HP panel bottom-right: name, level, gender, HP bar + HP numbers
- Bottom action panel: black background with white border, 4 buttons: FIGHT / BAG / POKEMON / RUN

**Battle Mechanics (Gen 3 strict)**:
- Physical/Special split by TYPE (not move category):
  - Special types: Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark
  - Physical types: Normal, Fighting, Flying, Poison, Ground, Rock, Bug, Ghost, Steel
- Damage formula: `floor(((floor(2*Lv/5+2) * Power * A/D) / 50) + 2) * Critical * STAB * Type1 * Type2 * random`
  - random = random integer 85-100 / 100
  - Critical = 2x
  - STAB = 1.5 if move type matches user type
  - Type effectiveness: 0.5/1/2
- Player Pokémon: Bulbasaur (id=1), Lv 5, use actual PokeAPI moves
- Enemy Pokémon: Charmander (id=4), Lv 5
- When player clicks FIGHT: show move selection (4 moves with PP)
- Enemy AI: choose super effective move if available; otherwise highest power; fallback to first move
- Turn order: speed stat comparison, ties random
- Animations: 
  - HP bar lerp animation (smooth fill/drain)
  - Hit shake animation on defending Pokémon sprite
  - Critical hit: brief yellow flash
  - Super effective: message in battle log
- Text: character by character typewriter effect in battle log
- Input locked during animations

**Asset Management**:
- AssetLoader class: loads sprites from PokeAPI GitHub URLs
- Display "Loading..." screen with progress bar while loading
- After load complete: show "Press ENTER to Start" on title
- Fallback: if image fails, draw colored rectangle placeholder

**Game Flow**:
1. Loading screen → 
2. Title/Press Start screen → 
3. Map exploration (walk around, find NPC or tall grass encounter) → 
4. Battle transition (GBA flash effect) → 
5. Battle scene → 
6. Victory/defeat → back to map

## Technical Requirements
- Single HTML file with embedded CSS and JS
- Code organized into classes: `AssetLoader`, `MapEngine`, `BattleEngine`, `GameLoop`, `DialogManager`
- Canvas-based rendering (use a main `<canvas>` element)
- Use PokeAPI for live Pokémon data: `https://pokeapi.co/api/v2/pokemon/{id}`
- Sprites from: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/`
- Use async/await for asset loading
- Handle CORS: PokeAPI sprites are publicly accessible

## Pokémon Data to fetch from PokeAPI
For Bulbasaur (player, id=1) and Charmander (enemy, id=4):
- stats: hp, attack, defense, special-attack, special-defense, speed
- moves: first 4 moves with their details (power, type, etc.)
- sprites: back sprite for player, front sprite for enemy

## Visual Style
- Gen 3 / GBA color palette: muted, warm tones
- HP bar: green → yellow (≤50%) → red (≤20%)
- Battle UI: classic black panels with white text
- Font: monospace or pixel-style (use "Courier New" or embed a pixel font via CSS)
- Map tiles: drawn with canvas rectangles/patterns, no external tile sheets needed

## Output
Save the complete, working game as `/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/game.html`

The file should be self-contained, working in any modern browser. No build tools needed.

{"mode": "run", "runTimeoutSeconds": 600, "runtime": "subagent", "task": "
Build a single-file HTML5 Pokémon Gen 3 style web battle game and save it as `/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/game.html`.

## Full Requirements

### Phase 1 - Map Engine
- Tile-based 2D map (20x15 tiles, 32px each), tiles: grass, tall grass, path/road, water
- Player character: drawn as a simple pixel-art sprite (colored rectangles)
- NPC Rival: drawn as a simple colored sprite
- Player moves with WASD/Arrow keys (tile-by-tile movement with smooth 8-frame interpolation)
- Collision: player cannot walk on water tiles or out of bounds
- Walking through tall grass has ~20% chance per step to trigger wild encounter
- NPC: approach within 1 tile distance and press Z to trigger dialog box
- Dialog box at bottom (Gen 3 style): text typewriter effect, advance with Z/Enter
- After NPC dialog ends → trigger battle transition → battle

### Phase 2 - Battle Engine
**Transition**: GBA-style white flash (screen → white → mosaic pixelate → battle scene)

**Battle Scene**:
- Canvas-drawn background: sky gradient top, brown/gray ground platforms
- Enemy Pokémon front sprite: top-right, on elevated platform
- Player Pokémon back sprite: bottom-left, on lower platform
- Enemy HP panel (top-left area): name, Lv, HP bar (trapezoidal Gen 3 style drawn on canvas)
- Player HP panel (bottom-right): name, Lv, HP bar + "HP: X/Y" numbers
- Bottom menu panel (black bg, white text): 
  - Main menu: FIGHT / BAG / POKEMON / RUN (2x2 grid)
  - Fight submenu: shows up to 4 moves with PP

**Gen 3 Battle Mechanics**:
- Physical/Special split by TYPE:
  - Special: Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark
  - Physical: Normal, Fighting, Flying, Poison, Ground, Rock, Bug, Ghost, Steel
- Damage formula: floor(((floor(2*Lv/5+2) * Power * A/D) / 50) + 2) * Critical * STAB * TypeEff * random/100
  - random = random int 85–100
  - Critical multiplier = 2
  - STAB = 1.5 if move type matches user type, else 1
  - TypeEff = 0.5 / 1 / 2 based on type chart
- Type effectiveness chart (Gen 1/3 style, use a hardcoded table)
- Player: Bulbasaur (id=1), Lv 5; Enemy: Charmander (id=4), Lv 5
- Stats scaled to Lv 5 using Gen 3 formula:
  - HP = floor((Base*2 + IV + floor(EV/4)) * Lv/100) + Lv + 10  (IV=15, EV=0)
  - Other = floor((Base*2 + IV + floor(EV/4)) * Lv/100) + 5  (IV=15, EV=0)
- Moves: fetch from PokeAPI, get 4 moves with power/type/PP
- Enemy AI: pick super effective move if available; else highest power; else first valid
- Animations:
  - HP bar smooth lerp (60fps requestAnimationFrame)
  - Hit shake: sprite offset oscillates 5 frames
  - Critical/super-effective messages in battle log
  - Text typewriter: 30ms per character
  - Input locked during all animations

**Asset Manager**:
- Load sprites from PokeAPI GitHub CDN (no API key needed):
  - Enemy front: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/{id}.png`
  - Player back: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/back/{id}.png`
- Fetch Pokémon data from: `https://pokeapi.co/api/v2/pokemon/{id}`
- Loading screen with progress bar
- "Press ENTER to Start" after load
- Fallback: colored rectangle if image fails

**Game Flow**:
1. Loading screen (fetch PokeAPI data + sprites)
2. "Press ENTER to Start" overlay
3. Map exploration
4. Battle transition (flash + pixelate)
5. Battle
6. Win/lose result text → back to map (R to restart)

## Code Structure
Organize into these classes/modules in the JS:
```
AssetLoader      - load images + fetch API data, track progress
MapEngine        - tile map, player/NPC movement, collision, encounter logic
BattleEngine     - battle state, damage calc, AI, animations
DialogManager    - typewriter dialog boxes
GameLoop         - main requestAnimationFrame loop, state machine
```

## Technical
- Single HTML file, embedded CSS + JS
- One `<canvas id="game">` element, 640x480 pixels
- CSS: center canvas, black background, pixel-art image-rendering
- Use async/await, Promises for loading
- Handle CORS (PokeAPI sprites are public CDN, no CORS issues)
- NO external dependencies (no jQuery, no frameworks)
- Must run in Chrome/Firefox from local file (file://) - avoid fetch for local files, only remote APIs

## Visual Details
- Map tile colors: grass=#4a8, tall grass=#2a6, path=#c9a84c, water=#449, wall=#888
- HP bar colors: green=#0f0 (>50%), yellow=#ff0 (20-50%), red=#f00 (<20%)
- Battle BG: sky #87ceeb top, ground #8b4513 bottom strip
- Font: "Courier New" monospace for all text
- Scale sprites 2x with canvas drawImage

## Save location
Write the complete file to: `/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/game.html`
"}

turns.content:
The file is complete and passes syntax validation. Here's a summary of what was built:

---

## Task Complete

**File saved:** `/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/game.html` (1374 lines, ~46KB)

### What was implemented:

**Phase 1 – Map Engine:**
- 20×15 tile map with grass, tall grass, path, and water tiles
- Pixel-art player (red cap trainer) and NPC rival (blue outfit) drawn via canvas rectangles
- WASD/Arrow key movement with smooth 8-frame interpolation
- Water tile + out-of-bounds collision
- 20% wild encounter chance on tall grass steps
- NPC proximity detection (1 tile) with Z/Enter to trigger dialog, "Z" speech bubble
- Gen 3 style dialog box with typewriter effect + blinking ▼ prompt
- NPC dialog → battle transition trigger

**Phase 2 – Battle Engine:**
- GBA-style white flash → hold → mosaic pixelate transition
- Canvas-drawn battle background: sky gradient + ground platforms
- Pokémon sprites from PokeAPI CDN (with colored rectangle fallback)
- Enemy HP panel (top-left) + Player HP panel (bottom-right) with HP numbers
- Smooth HP bar lerp animation (60fps)

## ✅ `game.html` is ready — 1,374 lines, ~46KB

Open it in any modern browser. Here's what's inside:

---

### Phase 1 — Map
- **20×15 tile map** with grass, tall grass, path, and water tiles
- **Player** (drawn pixel-art trainer) moves with WASD/Arrow keys, smooth 8-frame interpolation
- **Water & border collision** — can't walk off the map or into water
- **Tall grass encounters** — 20% chance per step triggers a wild battle
- **NPC Rival** — approach within 1 tile and press **Z** to start a typewriter dialog, which leads into battle
- **Gen 3-style dialog box** at the bottom with blinking ▼ prompt, advance with Z/Enter

---

### Phase 2 — Battle
- **GBA-style transition**: white flash → hold → mosaic pixelate dissolve into battle scene
- **Live PokeAPI data**: fetches Bulbasaur (player) and Charmander (enemy) stats + moves at startup
- **Sprites from PokeAPI CDN** — back sprite for player, front sprite for enemy; colored-rect fallback if load fails
- **Loading screen** with progress bar; "Press ENTER to Start" when ready
- **Gen 3 damage formula** implemented exactly per spec (including physical/special split by type, STAB, crits, type chart)
- **Full type chart** (all 18 types, Gen 3 values)
- **HP bar lerp animation**, hit shake, typewriter battle log
- **Enemy AI**: prioritizes super-effective moves → highest power → fallback
- **Action menu**: FIGHT / BAG / POKÉMON / RUN; Fight submenu shows moves with PP and type tag
- **Win/Lose/Run** result → press R to return to map
